explanation: The natural language requirement specifies that upon initiation of the Start-up Mode, the system must perform an initialization sequence involving loading default parameters and checking system memory, sensor and valve communications, as well as GUI-controller communications. Based on the atomic propositions provided, the most relevant events linked to these initialization tasks are the entry into StartUpMode (to serve as the trigger event), and the completion of initialization ('initDone'). Since checking communication with sensors and the GUI is explicitly included, the corresponding atomic propositions 'checkCommsSensors' and 'checkCommsGUI' are also relevant. The structured property is triggered 'upon StartUpMode'; the essential requirement is that 'initDone', 'checkCommsSensors', and 'checkCommsGUI' all occur as part of the initialization aftermath. This is best captured as: Upon StartUpMode being true, eventually all of the following must be satisfied (initDone, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsGUI). This translation models the sequencing concern at start-up and ensures all the specified checks are performed. The chosen template is: upon bool_exp1, eventually satisfy bool_exp3, where bool_exp1 is StartUpMode and bool_exp3 is (initDone & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsGUI).
decision1_substring: Upon initiation of the Start-up Mode
decision3_substring: the system shall go through an initialization process that loads default parameters and checks the system memory and the communication of the controller with the sensors and valves, as well as between the controller and the GUI.
output_structured_natural_language: upon StartUpMode, eventually satisfy initDone & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsGUI

This ensures that upon entering Start-up Mode, these initiation and communication checks happen and complete before initialization finishes.

explanation: The requirement states that once the Start-up Mode is initiated, the system must perform an initialization process, which involves loading default parameters and checking communication with sensors, valves, and the GUI. Given the provided atomic propositions, the most directly relevant ones are StartUpMode (indicates Start-up Mode is active), initStart (initialization start), initDone (initialization complete), loadLog (load parameters), checkCommsSensors (check/test communication with sensors), checkCommsValves (check/test communication with valves), checkCommsGUI (check/test communication with GUI), and initFail (indicates failure in initialization). To structurally capture the intent, we encode that upon entering StartUpMode, the initialization process (possibly represented by initStart) must be performed, and importantly, the sub-tasks of loading parameters and performing all the required checks must occur. Since there is no single atomic proposition covering all these initialization sub-tasks, we compose a boolean formula: loadLog & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI. Thus, the structured NL property uses the 'upon' template: upon StartUpMode, within N ticks, all these initialization actions are performed. As the original requirement does not specify a maximum duration, we do not set an N_DURATION. The substrings selected reflect where in the original NL each aspect comes from.
decision1_substring: Upon initiation of the Start-up Mode
decision3_substring: the system shall go through an initialization process that loads default parameters and checks the system memory and the communication of the controller with the sensors and valves, as well as between the controller and the GUI.
output_structured_natural_language: upon StartUpMode, eventually satisfy loadLog & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI

explanation: The requirement specifies that upon the initiation of Start-up Mode, the system must perform an initialization process. In this context, 'upon initiation of the Start-up Mode' corresponds to 'upon StartUpMode', and the system must go through a process that loads default parameters and checks the memory and communication with sensors, valves, and the GUI. From the atomic propositions, 'initStart' (indicates system/controller initialization start), 'loadLog' (indicates system load parameters), 'checkCommsSensors' (indicates check/test communication of the controller with the sensors), 'checkCommsValves' (indicates check/test communication of the controller with the valves), and 'checkCommsGUI' (indicates check/test communication between controller and GUI) closely match the described required actions. These actions should be initiated when StartUpMode starts, so the structured NL property uses the 'upon' template. The main intent is to trigger a set of checks and loading operations as soon as StartUpMode is initiated. Therefore, 'upon StartUpMode' (decision2) and 'immediately satisfy (initStart & loadLog & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI)' (decision3) best capture the requirement's intent.

decision3_substring: the system shall go through an initialization process that loads default parameters and checks the system memory and the communication of the controller with the sensors and valves, as well as between the controller and the GUI.

explanation: The requirement specifies that when the Start-up Mode is initiated, the system should perform an initialization sequence, which consists of loading default parameters, checking system memory, and verifying communication between the controller and both the sensors, valves, and the GUI. The atomic propositions provided relevant to this process are 'StartUpMode', 'initStart', 'loadLog', 'checkCommsSensors', and 'checkCommsGUI'. However, from the list the available atomic propositions, 'initStart' indicates the start of initialization, 'loadLog' denotes system loading parameters, 'checkCommsSensors' and 'checkCommsGUI' represent checks of communication, and 'initDone' represents that all configuration loading and internal checking is successfully finished. The structured natural language is thus: upon the Start-up Mode being activated ('upon StartUpMode'), the system should eventually carry out ('eventually satisfy') the conjunction of starting system initialization, loading parameters, checking communication with sensors, and checking communication with GUI, all of which can be written using the operators '&' and the relevant atomic propositions. This structured NL captures the requirement's idea that these steps must follow Start-up Mode initiation. The decision substrings are mapped to the phrases referring to when the action happens, what is included in the action, and the sequence of required checks.
